reviews.review_title: Challenging for people with speech issue
reviews.review_description:
This is an updated review.

I had a frustrating problem with the spaceship room. On the bridge you are required to speak a secret code. I tried countless times to get it to recognize the word I was speaking, without success. There is no alternative input method for this code. It finally recognized the code when I used the Dictonary app on my phone to say the code word. I was able to complete the room after days of messing with this one step. During my efforts, I restarted the room several times. Each time I was required to start from the beginning. Progress was not saved.

I give credit to the developer for offering to give me another of his escape rooms at no charge. It's a good gesture, but when I encounter an issue like this in a game, it only serves to remind me of my disability. I also have balance issues from the same medical event that affected by voice. I can't play any games that require standing and only run in immersive mode. VR is a wonderful technology, but like so many things in society, it's potential value is diminished by failing to address disability issues.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your thoughtful review and for giving my game a chance - I’m truly glad to hear you enjoyed the narration and your experience with the island escape room, and that you decided to explore the additional content as well.


I am very sorry for the frustration you encountered with the voice recognition requirement in the spaceship room. That’s absolutely not the experience I want for my players. Please note that the word “passcode” is NOT the correct input - I don’t want to reveal the actual word here, but I wanted to clarify this to avoid confusion for any other players reading this.


That being said, please know that I’m actively working on adding alternative input methods so that progression isn’t dependent on voice recognition alone. Your feedback is incredibly valuable in helping me identify and prioritize these improvements.


In the meantime, you can check your account through Meta to request a refund if this issue prevented you from enjoying the content as intended. Additionally, I’d be happy to offer you a code for another one of my escape rooms that doesn’t require voice input. Please contact me through team@dijla.ventures, and I’ll make sure you receive it.


Again, I truly appreciate your support and your detailed feedback – it helps me make the experience better for everyone.

reviews.review_description: I really liked the graphics and the interesting puzzles. Love the voice! Very clever game. Only issue I had was at the very end (otherwise I would’ve given five stars). I finished the puzzle. The voice told me I had finished and to come home to the ship. When I ducked to get under the door to go to the space pod door, I got stuck in the floor and couldn’t get out. I restarted the level got the message that I had completed all the puzzles and to come home to the ship, went out to the pod door and nothing happened. Am I missing a final puzzle?

developer_response.body:
thank you so much for the kind words - I really appreciate you taking the time to write this! And I’m very sorry you ran into that issue at the end. That definitely shouldn’t happen.

What you experienced sounds like a bug, not a missing puzzle. The final sequence should trigger when you return to the pod door, so getting stuck in the floor or having nothing happen there is not intended behavior. I’m currently looking into this issue and working on a fix so it doesn’t happen again.

Thank you again for playing and for the detailed report :)

reviews.review_title: We need more titles like this!
reviews.review_description:
This is a simple, smooth, classic escape room!
This is not a 5/5, but I give it trust, because it is very enjoyable and it is very cheap! 
The puzzles are quite easy, the voice is clear, the gameplay is good BUT it lacks a couple of things: subtitles (not anyone can understand only the English voice), no control configuration. (I hate teleport with left controller).
Please, make others rooms!
(I didn't play yet the Starship and I am scared since I am not a native English!)

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! I'm glad you enjoyed it. Subtitles and control options are great feedback and are on my radar :D

reviews.review_description:
I purchased the add on escape rooms (thankfully at deep discount). They’re okay, narrator is interesting, but some puzzles feel like they just went “eh, good enough”. But the biggest issue was at the very end, where I was supposed to escape the ship, I got tossed out into open space and the ship just… left me there. It ascended and there I am, floating in (admittedly beautiful) open space. So I tried again (no save checkpoint that I found, so basically do the whole game again), and then… floating in space once more. I assume that is not the intended ending, but I’m going to stay there. The people on that ship seem fairly incompetent anyway.

Edit: after developer response (I appreciate it) I went in and tried again. Pretty quick game when you know all the answers. I “knocked” in a different spot and managed to get to the next level. Not sure what about that made a difference; maybe there’s a specific spot, but it obviously wasn’t clear to me. At least I’m not floating in the cold emptiness of space anymore.

developer_response.body:
Update:
As of 2.1.2026, 1.1.5 fixes this issue! Also, thank you for the update on your end :D and I appreciate the community banding together to help me reproduce the issue.

Original response:
Hey! Thank you for taking the time to play and for writing such a detailed review. I’m genuinely sorry about the ending you ran into. That’s definitely not the intended experience, and I can understand how frustrating it must have been to get all the way there only to be ejected into space… twice.

I tried to reproduce the issue on my end and haven’t been able to yet, which makes it even more frustrating on my side, because it means something slipped through that I clearly didn’t catch. I’m going to spend more time digging into it next week to track down what’s happening and make sure it doesn’t occur again.

Cheers and have a happy new year :)

reviews.review_description: Only two levels are free?! whats the point?
I unlocked the island right away, but got stuck on the rows columns part… of course, when I got to get off VR I almost got to mind, what I would have needed to do. W game btw

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoyed it :D

reviews.review_description: Only one free level that takes less that 5 minutes, generic unity graphics, voice over is constant and most likely ai

developer_response.body:
youre right that the free portion is short. I designed it mainly as a small demo so players can get a feel for the experience before deciding whether it’s something they’d like to continue with. That said, I completely understand how it can feel limiting, and I’m actively thinking about ways to expand the free content in future updates.

As for aesthetic considerations, im a solo dev, so while not every choice will appeal to everyone, I tried to focus most on making the gameplay fun and engaging. I really appreciate you sharing how it came across to you, because that helps me make better decisions going forward.

reviews.review_description:
I did the demo with the 5 puzzles.

Layering those liquids was horrible for me (didn’t want to use a hint), but couldn’t solve it without 🤣🤣🤣.

Good that there is a storyline.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind review!! Haha yeah the liquids part was a bit tough -- I was trying to experiment with some new VR concepts 😅 Cheers and have an amazing new year!

reviews.review_description: Its very fun. But the drawer had me confused i didn't know when to close one and open another
have to pay to play easy escape rooms one out of five stars not that fun wouldn't recommend it 😅

reviews.review_title: I want to exit Exit Code
reviews.review_description: Only the tutorial and secret level are free. Both of which are too easy and not fun.

The tutorial and free game where short and sweet, the game play is a bit clunky, moving about was a bit difficult the graphics where ok, i liked the big space ship that came and stole you from the island, it wasn’t enough to make me want to purchase the second two rooms but it did keep me entertained for 10 mins 

I can’t seem to tap the stars to rate but i give it 3 stars ⭐️ 🌟 🌟

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for taking the time to play and leave feedback - I really appreciate it. This is actually my first game as an indie developer, so it was definitely a learning experience. I’m glad it was fun for those 10 mins!

reviews.review_description: i can’t even get the apple in the basket. as soon as i raise my hand, the basket raises more

developer_response.body: Hi there - thanks for giving the game a try, and I’m really sorry the tutorial felt frustrating. One thing that sometimes happens is that the joystick can adjust your in-game height if it’s nudged unintentionally, which can make the basket appear to rise or fall even when your hand stays level.

reviews.review_description: very very good i recommend

developer_response.body: tysm!! :D

reviews.review_description: It was really fun 😀😀

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind review!! I'm glad you had fun :D

reviews.review_description:
I like this game, it has enough good free content to experience the game before buying the dlc, smart puzzles and good hint feature. I liked the sense of humor of the hint too. puzzles aren't cheap or super hard.
Tldr:  I recommend it.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind review :)

reviews.review_description: Its ok, has potential, I see that it has the same assets as the break in vr and other game. More free maps would be good.

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks for the feedback and for giving it a try 🙂

youre right - as an indie dev I reuse some common assets to stay focused on building new gameplay and experiences. I’m definitely planning to add more free maps over time. Appreciate you checking it out!

reviews.review_description:
Really nice game for a quick sesion. 
Story? AMAZING!
really fun ways to escape!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words :D Glad you had fun!

reviews.review_description:
Super fun and i loved how if you dont get it. The voice will help
but there are only 1 free level but that does not ruin my experience awesome game

developer_response.body: Hey! Thank you SO much for the kind review. I'm happy you enjoyed the island!

reviews.review_description: The graphics and animations arent realistic ,but the're good. It was my first time doing an escape room. I ,liked the fact that there is story to it. Though the one bummer was that there are only 3 escape rooms. I love it if there more options.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thank you so much for the kind review! 😊 My love for real-life escape rooms is what pushed me to create Exit Code, so it genuinely means a lot to hear that you enjoyed the rooms.

As for more options, I’m a bit busy with life at the moment, but I highly recommend trying a close friend’s escape experience on here called “Escape Room Exit Condition One HD” (dev is Damon Poole). If you liked mine, I think you’ll love his too!

Cheers and happy holidays!

reviews.review_description: The game was so good

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind review :D

reviews.review_description: Had a great time playing this in early access! Both levels are fun. Loved the art style and the narrator. 10/10 if you like escape rooms!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! I’m glad you had fun playing :)

reviews.review_title: Huge Fun For a Small Price!
reviews.review_description: Loved these rooms. My only wish is that the dev adds more!! :)

developer_response.body: Thanks so much—really glad you had fun! More rooms are coming soon. 🙂

reviews.review_description: I rily liked the narrator lol. the free escape room has some cool concepts. nice that there are hint buttons in the game.

developer_response.body: Tysm!! I'm glad you enjoyed it!
